Base Tip Text: Notice how today feels different without the routines of home and work. What does that freedom reveal about what you truly want from your days?
Approach everything today as if you've never seen it before. Let go of assumptions and let the unfamiliar teach you something new.
Choose one thing today to experience at half your normal pace. What do you notice that you would have missed?
The things that surprise or frustrate you here often reflect something about your life at home. What is this place showing you?
Find 15 minutes alone today with no phone, no agenda. Just be where you are. What comes up?
Have a real conversation with someone local today. Ask them what they love most about where they live.
Do one thing today that makes you slightly uncomfortable. Growth lives just past the edge of comfort.
Close your eyes for a moment. What do you hear, smell, feel? Travel is a full-body experience — don't just see it.
Notice what worries or thoughts from home are following you. Are they necessary right now, or can you set them down for today?
Find a high point — a hill, a rooftop, a window — and look out. How does your life look from this distance?
Find beauty in something small and unremarkable today. A doorway, a meal, a shadow. What does it tell you?
If you're with someone, notice what this experience brings out in them that you don't normally see. What does that teach you about them — and you?
If you could bring one thing from this place back into your daily life, what would it be? Why don't you already have it?
Name three things about today — just today — that you are grateful for. Not the trip overall, just today.
You will go home soon. Who do you want to be when you get there? What will you do differently?
Think about things that are happening in your daily life and reflect on why they are that way.
What are some things that I can change in my life that extends what I can thrive with?

Tip Name: Best Version of Myself on Return
Base Tip Text: How can I become the best version of myself upon return home?
What can I learn from seeing myself through other people's eyes?
